key_insight: Randomizing Hamiltonian integration times lets RHMC mix faster for log-concave targets, with provable acceleration in KL divergence.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more efficient sampling strategy for high-dimensional uncertainty quantification and probabilistic inference when fast, reliable sampling from log-concave posteriors is needed.
method: Simulate continuous-time Hamiltonian dynamics for a random duration.
Reset velocity to an independent Gaussian between trajectories.
Use triangular or exponential integration-time distributions, including a schedule with increasing means for the non-strongly log-concave case.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theory-based convergence guarantees: exponential KL decay with total integration time O(alpha^{-1/2} log(epsilon^{-1})) under an alpha-Talagrand condition, and O(epsilon^{-1/2}) with increasing random times for general log-concave targets.
watch_item: The guarantees are theoretical and depend on log-concavity plus specific random-time choices, so practical performance may be sensitive to tuning and to how accurately Hamiltonian dynamics are numerically simulated.
paper_title: Accelerated Mixing Time of Randomized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
